attributes.description: Full URL to join the video meeting (Zoom, Teams, Meet, etc. join link).
attributes.name: meetingUrl
attributes.usage: The link participants click to join. Typically generated by the video-platform integration when the meeting is created. Drives one-click join from CRM and calendar.

attributes.name: platform
attributes.usage: Drives integration routing (which platform's API to query for recordings) and reporting on platform mix.

attributes.description: Native meeting identifier in the video platform (Zoom Meeting ID, Teams ID, etc.).
attributes.name: meetingId
attributes.usage: Used by integrations to fetch recordings, transcripts, and attendance reports from the platform's API after the meeting.

attributes.description: URL to the video recording (when capture is enabled).
attributes.name: recordingUrl
attributes.usage: Drives meeting-recording review workflows and AI-coaching pipelines (Gong, Chorus, Fathom). Often populated post-meeting by an integration.

attributes.description: URL to the meeting transcript (often AI-generated).
attributes.name: transcriptUrl
attributes.usage: Drives full-text search across meeting content, AI summary generation, and compliance audit logs.

attributes.description: Contacts who attended (or were invited to) the video meeting.
attributes.name: attendees
attributes.usage: Multi-attendee list. Drives engagement-frequency calculations and post-meeting follow-up assignment.

business_context: Video Meeting records have emerged as a distinct subclass since the pandemic-driven shift to remote work made video calls the default rather than the exception for many sales, fundraising, and program conversations. They differ from in-person Meeting records in three ways: (1) location is a URL rather than a physical address; (2) a platform identifier (Zoom / Teams / Meet / Webex) drives integration behavior; (3) recording and transcript artifacts are routinely generated and need linkage to the meeting record. Most surveyed systems don't natively split video from in-person meetings — they use one Meeting / Event entity and accommodate video URLs via custom fields or platform integrations. The catalog separates them at the modeling level so the data shape is honest about the channel difference, even when the deploy-time implementation uses one shared Meeting / Event entity. AI-meeting-assistant tools (Otter, Fathom, Fireflies) and revenue-intelligence platforms (Gong, Chorus) all ingest video-meeting recordings as a primary signal source — making the recording-URL and transcript-URL attributes increasingly important. When interviewing clients, surface "Video Meeting" by asking whether they need to track meeting platform, recording URLs, or transcripts separately from in-person meetings.
name: Video Meeting (Activity subclass)
purpose: A scheduled video conference (Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Google Meet, Webex, etc.) with one or more contacts. A subclass of Activity that adds video-specific delta attributes: meeting URL, platform identifier, meeting ID, optional recording URL, optional transcript URL.

sources.title: Attio: Add record activities (calendar sync detects video meetings)

systems.name: Meeting with auto-detected video link
systems.notes: Attio recognizes video-meeting URLs in calendar events and surfaces platform metadata. No first-class Video Meeting entity.

systems.name: Interaction (subtype=Phone Call or custom Video)
systems.notes: Bloomerang doesn't have a Video Meeting subtype natively. Tracked as Phone Call or Other Interaction with custom field for video-meeting URL.

systems.name: Activity (activity_type=Meeting) with custom fields
systems.notes: CiviCRM Activity with custom fields for video-meeting URL and platform.

systems.name: Meeting with custom video fields
systems.notes: EspoCRM Meeting entity with custom fields for video URL and platform. No native split.

systems.name: Meeting with platform integration
systems.notes: HubSpot Meeting carries video-meeting URL via Zoom / Teams / Google Meet integrations. Distinct subclass not modeled natively but commonly added via custom property.

systems.name: Event with custom video fields
systems.notes: No native split from in-person Event. Customers add custom fields for VideoMeetingUrl, Platform, RecordingUrl. Salesforce Einstein Conversation Insights surfaces video recordings on linked Events.

systems.name: Event with custom video fields
systems.notes: Same as Salesforce. Donor-cultivation video meetings tracked as Events with custom video-URL fields.
